Est. 2022 · Omaha, NE

Grammercy Park
Neighborhood Collective

No fees, no dues, just solidarity.

We're a group of neighbors working to build a more connected, more engaged community in Omaha. Plant swaps, block parties, clean ups, housing advocacy — we go where the community leads. We primarily serve neighbors between Benson and Dundee, east of Country Club, but anyone is welcome.

What affects one of us affects us all.

Join the Collective What's Happening
Events & Meetings

What's Coming Up

🌱 May 9 — Spring Plant Swap Saturday, May 9 · 2:00–4:00 PM · Rain date: May 23

We're partnering with Gina and Omaha Plant Addicts this year! No plants? Take ours! Have extras? Bring them. All plants, seeds, starters, and garden items welcome. Please label your stuff! No sales! Bring a table if you have a lot. Leftovers go to Benson Plant Rescue.

Sign up for the address →

Open Now · Deadline June 30, 2026

Our First-Ever Design Contest

Our Collective grew out of plant swaps, potlucks, and people who care about their neighbors and the flora and fauna of this neighborhood. We want a design that captures that: something real, handmade-feeling, and alive that could live on a t-shirt and grow into a GPC logo.

The winning design will:

  • Be rooted in what makes this community feel like home — diverse people, plants, animals, good neighbors, a sense of place
  • Work in a single color and in full color — and scale from a t-shirt to stickers or other merch
  • Format: JPG, SVG, or PDF · You can enter more than once
  • Prize: $100 gift card to Blue Line Coffee / Green Line Cafe · Merch printed by PKP Screenprinting
Submit Your Design →
What We Do

Projects & Advocacy

We work with neighbors and the city on issues that shape everyday life here — everything from broken streetlights to feeding our neighbors and advocating for better policies that govern our community.

  • Block parties, plant swaps, and events that build real community
  • Affordable housing policies and protections for long-term residents
  • Community gardens and shared green spaces
  • TIF development projects in our neighborhood — tracking closely

Omaha TIF Tracker

Tax Increment Financing redirects increased property taxes from new development back to developers instead of schools, parks, and public services. An unintended consequence: homeowners near TIF projects often see their own taxes rise to offset the gap.

$748MRefunded to developers
$122MPaid into public financing
View Omaha TIF Database → Current City Council Agenda →
Get Involved

Join Us

We're a DIY community group and we need you. Spread the word to your neighbors, pitch in your time or skills, help us grow, show up — we go where the community leads. Every bit counts.

Become a Block Connector

GPC is non-hierarchical — we go where the community leads us. Block Connectors are how we make that real. You'd be our point person on your block: we send you updates, resources, and things worth sharing, and you get them to your neighbors however works best for you. A text thread, a flyer on a door, a conversation over the fence.

No meetings required. Just a neighbor who gives a damn.

Join the Collective Become a Block Connector
Email Us Facebook

Just moved to the neighborhood?
Becoming a Block Connector is the fastest way to meet the people on your block.

Local Contacts & Links

Resources

City services, utilities, and emergency contacts for residents.
